corelogic_v10523

Let $|A|=2$, $|B|=4$, $|C|=2$.

•  $R\subseteq A\times B$ is an injective total map $A\to B$; write $Y=\operatorname{rng}(R)$ (so $|Y|=2$).  
•  $S\subseteq B\times C$ is a single-valued surjection $B\to C$ such that each $c\in C$ has exactly two pre-images in $B$.

Define the relative product  
$T = R \mid S = \{(a,c)\in A\times C : \exists b\,(aRb\land bSc)\}$.

How many distinct relations $T$ can arise as $(R,S)$ vary over all maps of the stated kinds?  Give a single integer.

By Principia Mathematica §43·2, $\,(R\!\upharpoonright Y)\mid(S\!\upharpoonright Y) = (R\mid S)\!\upharpoonright Y$.  Since $R$ already lands in $Y$, this shows $T$ is determined solely by $S$ restricted to $Y$.  An onto map $Y\to C$ with $|Y|=|C|=2$ has exactly two possible shapes (identity or swap).  Both occur, because for either choice of $S\!\upharpoonright Y$ we may extend $S$ arbitrarily on $B\setminus Y$ without affecting $T$.

2
